camp_guidelines_2026.page_title: Camp Guidelines — 3rd Gangjeong Peace & Music Camp
camp_guidelines_2026.page_subtitle: For a Safe and Dignified Camp
camp_guidelines_2026.seo_title: Camp Guidelines | 3rd Gangjeong Peace & Music Camp 2026
camp_guidelines_2026.seo_description: Camp guidelines for the 3rd Gangjeong Peace & Music Camp. Prohibited behaviors, consent, reporting procedures, organizer response, medical emergencies, outdoor safety, accessibility, and environmental policy.
camp_guidelines_2026.summary_heading: For a Safe and Dignified Camp
camp_guidelines_2026.summary_intro: The Gangjeong Peace & Music Camp is a place where everyone participates safely and with dignity. These guidelines apply equally to musicians, staff, volunteers, and audience members.
camp_guidelines_2026.intro_body: The Gangjeong Peace & Music Camp is a space of musical solidarity, dreaming of a world without war. These guidelines exist so that every participant — musician, staff, volunteer, or audience member — can share this time safely and with dignity. They apply equally to all.
camp_guidelines_2026.prohibited_intro: The following behaviors are strictly prohibited throughout the event, regardless of location.
prohibited_hate_items: Any discriminatory or hateful speech and behavior based on gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, nationality, ethnicity, disability, appearance, religion, class, or other grounds. Being "just a joke" is not an excuse.
prohibited_harassment_items: Unwanted physical contact (including minor contact such as handshakes or hugs)
Sexual remarks, jokes with sexual connotations, or comments on someone's appearance or body
Approaching someone without consent, following them, or repeatedly contacting them
Sexual harassment through digital or online means
prohibited_filming_items: Taking or posting photos or videos that identify someone's face without prior notice or consent
Illegal filming (of bodies, private spaces, etc.)
camp_guidelines_2026.consent_body: Consent must be clear, voluntary, and revocable at any time. "No" or "I'm uncomfortable" is a complete answer in itself — no explanation is required. Silence, hesitation, or uncertainty is not consent. Past consent does not guarantee present consent. Consent given while intoxicated may not be valid.
camp_guidelines_2026.bystander_title: 4. If You Experience or Witness an Uncomfortable Situation
bystander_items: Intervene directly — step in naturally by changing the subject or interrupting the conversation.
Check on the person — ask "Are you okay?" and stay with them.
Seek help — alert a nearby staff member.
Document — if it is safe to do so, note the time, place, and details of the situation.
camp_guidelines_2026.report_intro: If you experience or witness an incident, please reach out through any of the following means.
report_items: Contact a staff member in person, by phone, or by message
Gender Equality Manager (sexual violence): Yum (010-8170-2188), Lee Sang (010-2379-0760)
Reports are accepted even after the event has ended.
response_harassment_items: The reporter or survivor will be connected with a staff member.
The survivor and the accused will be separated.
The survivor will be supported in moving to a safe location of their choosing.
camp_guidelines_2026.response_options_label: With the survivor's consent, the following options will be offered. No option will be forced.
response_options_items: Receive on-site support from the safety team
Assistance in filing a police report (112)

response_hotlines.name: Women's Emergency Hotline
response_hotlines.desc: Emergency counseling for sexual/domestic violence, protection referral

response_hotlines.name: Korea Sexual Violence Relief Center
response_hotlines.desc: Psychological support and legal guidance for sexual violence

response_hotlines.name: Jeju Sexual Violence Counseling Center
response_hotlines.desc: On-site sexual violence support in Jeju

response_hotlines.name: Mental Health Crisis Hotline
response_hotlines.desc: Emergency counseling for mental health crises and psychological distress

camp_guidelines_2026.response_assault_subtitle: In cases of sexual assault or rape
response_assault_items: The reporter or survivor will be connected with a staff member.
The survivor and the accused will be separated immediately.
The survivor will be supported in moving to a safe location of their choosing.
Police (112) will be contacted to ensure the strongest possible separation measure and legal evidence.
response_evidence_items: Do not wash immediately after the incident. Place the clothing worn in a paper bag and visit a Sunflower Center or gynecology clinic for DNA collection.
Preserve KakaoTalk messages, emails, CCTV footage, and witness statements from before and after the incident.
Write a detailed record of the date, location, specific actions, and the perpetrator's reactions.
response_other_items: The reporter or survivor will be connected with the safety team.
A course of action will be developed through communication between the reporter or survivor and the staff member.
response_perpetrator_items: The status of the accused (musician, staff, audience, etc.) is not grounds for exception.
The level of action taken is at the discretion of staff and authorities, and cannot be contested on-site.
response_confidentiality_items: The survivor's identity and details of the incident will be shared only with the minimum number of people necessary for response.
Nothing will be disclosed externally without the survivor's consent.
response_secondary_harm_items: Questions such as "Why were you there?" or "Why didn't you say something sooner?" will not be asked.
Defending the accused or expressing doubt about the survivor will be treated as secondary harm.
medical_items: In an emergency, call 119 immediately and notify a nearby staff member.
Basic first-aid supplies will be available at the main booth.
outdoor_items: In the event of severe weather such as a typhoon or heavy rain, follow organizer instructions and evacuate immediately.
Regarding the Gangjeon Stream — swimming while intoxicated is at your own risk; children must be accompanied by a guardian at all times.
The area near the entrance may be congested due to construction on the Gangjeon Bridge. We recommend using public transportation or carpooling.
Lost items will be kept at the organizer's booth for one week after the event, then disposed of.
Guardians are responsible for the safety of children in their care. Do not leave children unattended.

If an adult who is not a child's guardian is alone with a child in a way that feels unnatural, please notify a staff member. Inappropriate words or physical contact toward children will be handled more seriously than similar incidents involving adults.
accessibility_items: We have not been able to arrange separate interpretation, sign language interpretation, or audio description services.
We will endeavor to operate all public restrooms as gender-neutral facilities during performance hours.
camp_guidelines_2026.environment_intro: Gangjeong village is a space that has defended ecology and peace against militarization. We have a responsibility to preserve this land in its entirety.
environment_items: Take your trash with you. Use the recycling bins and do not leave waste at the venue.
Do not damage the natural environment within the venue (plants, rocks, waterways, etc.).
Minimize the use of disposable items. Bringing your own cup and utensils is encouraged.
Do not use soap or detergents when playing in the Gangjeong Stream.
Park only in designated parking areas.
camp_guidelines_2026.contact_note: Inquiries and suggestions before the camp only.
